tasks.title: Add try outcome lifecycle records
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 1.1-1.6. This is the lifecycle foundation for the rest of the change. Read proposal.md, design.md, specs/relay-runner/spec.md, and tasks.md before editing.

Outcome: Rally has a first-class TryOutcome type with completed, handoff_requested, incomplete, run_timeout, handoff_timeout, failed, and interrupted values; helpers cover success and terminal-for-run behavior; store.TryRecord retains Completed and gains Outcome and HandoffOnly; runOutcome can surface the resolving try's Outcome alongside FailureCategory.

Files & scope: internal/reliability/ or internal/store/ for the TryOutcome type, internal/store/records.go, internal/relay/runner.go, internal/reliability/category.go only to prove it stays unchanged, and focused tests. Do not implement timeout timers or recovery routing in this lap.

Design: Keep TryOutcome orthogonal to FailureCategory. Do not add run_timeout or handoff_* to FailureCategory. Only OutcomeFailed carries a FailureCategory. Start moving success/freeze/Issue/retry decisions toward TryOutcome where needed for this slice, but avoid broad runner rewrites beyond the lifecycle plumbing needed to compile and test.

Acceptance: Tests prove handoff_requested is success-side and non-freezing, run_timeout is non-freezing/non-Issue/not a recovery trigger by itself at the model level, handoff_timeout is non-freezing and terminal for a run, failed outcomes still carry a FailureCategory, and FailureCategory has no handoff_* or run_timeout values. Run targeted reliability/store/relay tests. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Add reliability timeout configuration
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 2.1-2.4. This builds on the TryOutcome record lap but should stay focused on config parsing/defaulting and the interactive form.

Outcome: [reliability] accepts RunTimeoutSecs, TryTimeoutSecs, and HandoffTimeoutSecs with defaults 4500, 3600, and 300 when unset or zero; RunnerConfig exposes the effective values; the config form includes all three fields in the reliability group.

Files & scope: internal/config/config_v2.go and tests, internal/cli/config.go and tests, runner config construction only where required to pass values through. Do not implement timer behavior in runner.go.

Design: Validate or clamp so handoff_timeout_secs never reaches or exceeds the effective try/run bounds. If try_timeout_secs >= run_timeout_secs, accept the config and let the run budget subsume the per-try cap rather than returning an error. Preserve existing reliability behavior for stall_threshold_secs, retry_budget, and liveness_probe.

Acceptance: Tests cover unset/zero defaults, configured values, handoff window at or above try/run bounds, and try cap at or above run budget. Run go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/cli/.... Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify lifecycle and timeout configuration
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against add-recovery-role-timeouts, then focus on tasks 1.1-1.6 and 2.1-2.4 only.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target from the user, PR metadata, repo docs, branch config, or recent history, and use that target in diff commands. Identify the first lap/try in this batch and treat earlier branch work as pre-existing unless the user asks otherwise. Do not rewrite git history.

Review with depth: confirm TryOutcome is a lifecycle dimension and FailureCategory remains a failure-cause taxonomy; inspect every decision site touched for success/freeze/Issue/retry behavior; confirm store JSON compatibility and RunnerConfig defaulting/clamping semantics; check the interactive config form names and persistence.

Acceptance: Run targeted store/reliability/config/cli/relay tests relevant to this slice. Check off only tasks 1 and 2 boxes that are verified correct and sufficiently covered. Fix only tiny safe one-liners inline; larger gaps become focused head laps.

tasks.title: Enforce run and try timeout budgets
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 3.1-3.4. The risky boundary is internal/relay/runner.go around runOne and runActionLoop.

Outcome: runOne tracks a per-run wall-clock budget across all retry attempts, measured from run start and excluding later bounded handoff work; runActionLoop can cancel an active attempt when the shared run deadline or per-try cap fires; post-loop handling distinguishes timeout outcomes from stalls and ordinary agent errors.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go and narrowly required helper/test files. Do not implement handoff-only resume in this lap except for clean interfaces/placeholders needed by the next lap. Do not change mid-run failover behavior.

Design: Construct the per-run deadline once before the attempt loop and pass the same deadline channel into every runActionLoop invocation. The per-try cap may be created per attempt. On run or try timeout, cancel attemptCtx, set explicit timeout state, drain tryCh, and let whichever of run budget, try cap, or stall fires first win. A per-try cap with run budget remaining may retry; run-budget exhaustion stops retries and proceeds to the handoff path in the next lap.

Acceptance: Focused tests or test scaffolding prove cumulative run time can stop retries, a per-try cap can end one attempt while leaving budget for retry, ordinary successful runs are unaffected, and stall precedence remains intact. Run targeted relay tests. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Complete timeout enforcement regression tests
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, task 3.5. The previous lap wires timer behavior; this lap broadens confidence around the runner timing state machine.

Outcome: The relay test suite covers cumulative retry time crossing run_timeout_secs, a single attempt crossing try_timeout_secs while run budget remains, under-budget completion, and stall firing before either hard timeout.

Files & scope: internal/relay tests and minimal testability hooks if the previous lap did not already add them. Avoid production changes unless tests expose a small obvious bug in the timeout wiring.

Design: Prefer fake clock, injected sleeper, or already-established runner test harness patterns over real multi-second sleeps. Tests should assert outcomes and retry behavior, not only elapsed wall time. Keep failure messages explicit about whether run timeout, try timeout, or stall won.

Acceptance: Targeted relay tests are deterministic and green. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Implement bounded handoff-only continuation
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 4.1-4.3 plus the handoff-only execution side of agent-lifecycle. This depends on timeout budget state and TryOutcome records.

Outcome: On run-budget exhaustion, a resumable harness with a captured session persists the cancelled implementation attempt as OutcomeRunTimeout, then runs one bounded handoff-only executeTry with ResumeSessionID, no stall detector, and a fresh attempt context limited by HandoffTimeout. The continuation is a separate TryRecord with the same RunID, next AttemptNumber even if maxAttempts+1, HandoffOnly=true, and the resolving outcome. No resume/no session records a single resolving OutcomeHandoffTimeout implementation try with a clear no-resume/no-session reason.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go, executor plumbing only as needed to reuse existing resume support, store record construction, and agent prompt accessor calls if already available. Prompt content itself is owned by a later prompt lap unless needed for compilation.

Design: OutcomeHandoffRequested requires a durable current-run progress.HandoffEntry appended after summaryEntryCountBeforeRun, proving both laps handoff and laps wrapup completed. Use transient HandoffState only to detect partial/no-wrapup attempts. OutcomeRunTimeout is observability for the cancelled implementation attempt and must not be treated as the run resolver when a handoff-only try follows.

Acceptance: Tests or focused harness fakes cover resumable continuation, no-resume/no-session, handoff+wrapup success, partial/no-wrapup failure, failed/timed-out continuation, next attempt numbering, and no freeze-counter increment. Run targeted relay/progress/store tests. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify timeout and bounded handoff behavior
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against add-recovery-role-timeouts, then focus on tasks 3.1-3.5 and 4.1-4.4.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target and first lap/try in this batch. Treat older branch work as pre-existing. Do not rewrite git history.

Trace the actual control flow in runner.go: one run deadline across retries, per-try cap scope, cancellation/drain behavior, stall precedence, no timer reset across retries, handoff-only resume preconditions, separate TryRecord ordering, durable handoff entry detection, no synthetic continuation when no resume/session exists, and freeze/Issue behavior for timeout outcomes.

Acceptance: Run deterministic relay tests covering timer and handoff-only paths plus any relevant progress/store tests. Check off only tasks 3 and 4 boxes that are verified correct and sufficiently covered. Tiny one-liner fixes only inline; substantive gaps become head laps.

tasks.title: Persist recovery trigger metadata
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 5.1-5.5 and 6.1. This lap establishes the data recorded by a resolving run before routing consumes it.

Outcome: runOne computes TryOutcome and DirtyHandoff using the current-run durable handoff entry plus hasOwnUncommittedChanges, surfaces outcome and lap ID on runOutcome, suppresses auto-commit on dirty handoff, and persists ResolvedRoute, DirtyHandoff, and HandoffCreatedLapIDs on TryRecord while preserving LapAssignee as the original queue assignee.

Files & scope: internal/store/records.go, internal/relay/runner.go, progress handoff entry use, and focused tests. Do not implement the store query or route substitution in this lap.

Design: Dirty handoff is not the incomplete outcome; any handoff makes incomplete unreachable through finalized. Ordinary failed outcomes are not recovery triggers. Clean handoff and incomplete-alone paths must remain compatible with existing follow-up and finalization retry behavior. Copy HandoffCreatedLapIDs from the durable handoff entry, not transient state.

Acceptance: Tests cover dirty handoff vs clean handoff vs incomplete-alone vs ordinary failed; dirty handoff leaves the working tree dirty and skips auto-commit; clean handoff behavior is unchanged; TryRecord captures resolved route and handoff-created lap IDs. Run targeted relay/store/progress tests. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Route recovery from persisted try records
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 6.2-6.6. This is the durable recovery-pending query and route substitution layer.

Outcome: For a claimed, not-done lap, Rally derives recovery-pending from tries.jsonl by reading the resolving try of the most-recent run: the handoff-only continuation try when one exists, otherwise the last try of the highest RunID for that original LapID. It triggers on OutcomeHandoffTimeout or DirtyHandoff, treats HandoffCreatedLapIDs head followups as recovery-continuation targets for the same dirty tree, resolves the recovery route by substituting Assignee: recovery, exposes effective assignee/prompt role recovery, preserves relay-wide --route precedence, and never mutates .laps/laps.json.

Files & scope: internal/store query code, internal/relay/route_runtime.go, route selection call sites, runner selection metadata, and tests. Do not change work-queue assignees.

Design: Count the anti-loop cap by distinct consecutive RunIDs whose resolving try has ResolvedRoute == recovery; do not count raw try rows and do not use LapAssignee. Allow at most 2. On cap hit, stop forcing recovery, raise a relay-synthesized needs_user operator Issue, and fall back to the normal route without writing RecoveryClassification. If no recovery route is configured, warn and fall back rather than deadlocking.

Acceptance: Tests prove dirty handoff and handoff_timeout route to recovery; a dirty handoff head followup routes to recovery using the original trigger; ordinary failed does not; state survives a simulated relay restart; missing recovery route warns/falls back; recovery prompt/effective role is recovery while LapAssignee remains original; the third consecutive recovery trigger stops at cap and raises needs_user. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Fix persisted recovery routing...
tasks.description: Fix persisted recovery routing to distinguish runs across relay restarts (use relay-aware run identity/order in internal/store/recovery.go) and add multi-relay regression coverage for resolver selection and recovery-cap counting.

tasks.title: Fix dirty-handoff derivation a...
tasks.description: Fix dirty-handoff derivation across retries: base recovery trigger and auto-commit suppression on run-scoped own-dirty detection, and add retry-then-handoff regression coverage in internal/relay/runner_outcome_test.go.

tasks.title: Verify recovery trigger routing
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against add-recovery-role-timeouts, then focus on tasks 5.1-5.5 and 6.1-6.7.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target and first lap/try in this batch. Treat pre-batch work as pre-existing. Do not rewrite git history.

Trace the core dependency chain: durable HandoffEntry detection, hasOwnUncommittedChanges, auto-commit suppression, TryRecord fields, resolving-try selection, HandoffCreatedLapIDs handling, routeRuntime assignee substitution, effective prompt role, relay-wide override precedence, missing recovery route fallback, and anti-loop cap Issue behavior.

Acceptance: Run targeted relay/store/routing tests. Check off only tasks 5 and 6 boxes after code inspection plus tests. Fix only tiny safe one-liners inline; larger gaps become focused head laps.

tasks.title: Add recovery and handoff prompts
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 7.1-7.5 and the prompt side of agent-lifecycle. This lap owns generic prompt assets and route config defaults.

Outcome: internal/agent_prompt/roles/recovery.md exists and is loadable/overridable like other roles; it contains the five-way classification contract and classify-then-act guidance while staying OpenSpec-agnostic. A general handoff-only snippet forbids implementation and directs blocker summary plus laps handoff and laps wrapup. Implementation role snippets junior/senior/ui include the five-iteration voluntary handoff rule; verify and recovery do not. The config form fixed role list includes recovery, the custom-route sort bound is updated, and defaults seed a senior-class recovery route.

Files & scope: internal/agent_prompt/roles and general snippets, agent_prompt loaders/accessors/tests, internal/cli/config.go route role list/defaults, default config seeding, and prompt composition tests. Do not add OpenSpec-specific text to generic role docs.

Design: The classification instruction must live only in roles/recovery.md so composed prompts mention laps wrapup --classification only when the effective assignee/prompt role is recovery. The voluntary handoff guidance belongs in implementation roles individually, not a shared snippet that would inject into VERIFY/RECOVERY.

Acceptance: Tests cover Role(recovery)/Role(RECOVERY), on-disk override, all five classifications in the snippet, handoff-only prompt forbids implementation, implementation prompts include five-iteration guidance, VERIFY/RECOVERY prompts omit that guidance, and non-recovery prompts omit recovery classification instructions. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Persist recovery wrapup classification
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 8.1-8.3. This lap wires the classification capture channel through existing progress and wrapup plumbing.

Outcome: progress.RunEntry has Classification; rally progress accepts --classification and persists it for both done and handoff wrapups; laps wrapup forwards the value; store.TryRecord has RecoveryClassification; runOne reads the current-run summary entry, validates {continue, discard, course_correct, repair_plan, needs_user}, stores it for recovery runs, and leaves it empty for non-recovery or omitted/unrecognised values without failing the run.

Files & scope: internal/progress/store.go, internal/progress/cli.go, laps hook/wrapup integration if it lives in this repo, internal/store/records.go, internal/relay/runner.go, and tests. Avoid changing the recovery prompt content except for naming mismatches.

Design: Reuse the wrapup channel rather than adding a side channel. Classification applies to recovery runs whether they finish with laps done or laps handoff. Validation is best-effort and must never make an otherwise valid run fail.

Acceptance: Tests prove valid classification round-trips from laps wrapup --classification to TryRecord for recovery completion and recovery handoff, non-recovery runs leave it empty, and invalid/omitted classification leaves it empty without failure. Run progress/store/relay tests. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify prompt and classification plumbing
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against add-recovery-role-timeouts, then focus on tasks 7.1-7.5 and 8.1-8.3.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target and first lap/try in this batch. Treat prior branch work as pre-existing. Do not rewrite git history.

Inspect composed prompts, not just source snippets: verify RECOVERY role is distinct from SENIOR/VERIFY, classification text is role-scoped to recovery, the handoff-only snippet cannot invite implementation, the five-iteration rule is only in junior/senior/ui, config route lists/defaults include recovery correctly, and the wrapup classification path works for both done and handoff.

Acceptance: Run targeted agent_prompt/progress/store/relay/config tests. Check off only tasks 7 and 8 boxes that are verified correct and sufficiently covered. Tiny one-liner fixes only inline; larger gaps become head laps.

tasks.title: Emit outcome and recovery telemetry tags
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 9.1-9.4. This lap extends telemetry with TryOutcome and RecoveryClassification without changing the failure taxonomy.

Outcome: FailureState has Outcome and RecoveryClassification; FailureStateTags emits an outcome scalar tag on every try event and the existing failure_category tag only for OutcomeFailed; recovery-route try events attach recovery_classification when recorded; run_timeout, handoff_timeout, and handoff_requested are spans/logs rather than Issues; needs_user from a recovery classification or recovery cap may capture an operator Issue; optional recovery_started common log may be added if it fits existing telemetry patterns.

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/failure_state.go, tags.go, sinks/tests, internal/relay telemetry call sites, and cap-hit Issue integration. Do not introduce a parallel category tag.

Design: Read outcome/classification from upstream try/run records or FailureState inputs; telemetry must not re-classify. Handoff-only continuation tries should be identifiable in structured try logs/spans. The other four recovery classifications remain spans/logs. Preserve existing Sentry grouping behavior for real infra and agent failures.

Acceptance: Tests cover outcome tags for run_timeout/handoff_timeout/handoff_requested, no Issue capture for those outcomes, failure_category only on failed outcome, handoff-only try identification, recovery_classification tag on recovery tries, and needs_user Issue behavior. Run go test ./internal/telemetry/... ./internal/relay/.... Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Document recovery timeouts and bump version
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec add-recovery-role-timeouts, tasks 10.1-10.2. The feature implementation should be in place before this lap.

Outcome: internal/buildinfo/VERSION is bumped to 0.9.0, and user-facing docs describe the RECOVERY role, per-run/per-try timeout behavior, bounded handoff-only resume, the two recovery triggers, anti-loop cap behavior at a user-visible level, recovery classification, and the reliability config keys run_timeout_secs, try_timeout_secs, and handoff_timeout_secs.

Files & scope: internal/buildinfo/VERSION, README.md and existing role/reliability docs if present. Avoid changing code beyond doc example naming mismatches or version tests.

Design: Keep docs OpenSpec-agnostic. Explain that RECOVERY is Rally routing/prompt behavior and that laps remains the queue backend. Do not instruct users to create v-tags manually; follow AGENTS.md release guidance.

Acceptance: Docs match implemented names and defaults. Version-related tests or checks pass. Do not check off tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify telemetry, docs, and release version
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against add-recovery-role-timeouts, then focus on tasks 9.1-9.4 and 10.1-10.2.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target and first lap/try in this batch. Treat earlier branch work as pre-existing. Do not rewrite git history.

Trace telemetry from TryOutcome/RecoveryClassification production through FailureStateTags, Sentry Issue decisions, structured logs/spans, and cap-hit needs_user behavior. Confirm docs and VERSION match the implemented behavior and AGENTS.md release guidance.

Acceptance: Run targeted telemetry/relay/version/doc checks as appropriate. Check off only tasks 9 and 10 boxes that are verified correct and sufficiently covered. Tiny one-liner fixes only inline; larger gaps become focused head laps.

tasks.title: Final full-change verification for recovery role timeouts
tasks.description:
Run the complete openspec-verify-change skill for add-recovery-role-timeouts and inspect the whole diff.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target from the user, PR metadata, repo docs, branch config, or recent history, and use that target in diff commands. Identify the first lap/try in this batch and treat earlier branch work as pre-existing unless the user asks otherwise. Do not rewrite git history.

Review the whole outcome with depth: TryOutcome and FailureCategory separation; run/try timeout deadlines across retries; bounded handoff-only continuation as a separate TryRecord; durable handoff detection; dirty-handoff auto-commit suppression; persisted recovery-pending routing and anti-loop cap; effective RECOVERY prompt role; classification through wrapup; telemetry outcome/classification tags; docs; version bump.

Acceptance: Run targeted suites plus full go test ./... and go build ./... if feasible. Check off any remaining tasks.md boxes only for verified-correct work. For substantive gaps, add focused head laps using additive/revert commits only; do not turn final verification into a hidden implementation phase.

tasks.title: Make telemetry Sink boundary backend-neutral and add LevelWarning
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 1.2, 1.4, 1.7). Prepare the sink boundary so the New Relic sink can be added next without leaking Sentry concepts. Sentry MUST keep compiling — do NOT delete sentry.go or the dependency yet (that happens in the cut-over lap).

Outcome: Sink/Span/FailureEvent/Event doc comments in internal/telemetry/sink.go are backend-neutral ("operator-worthy failure", "custom event", "flush") with no "Sentry Issue" wording. Scrubbing in scrubber.go is available as backend-neutral helpers that sanitize Rally-supplied scalar attributes and string-keyed context maps, independent of any sentry.Event type. A new telemetry.LevelWarning EventLevel exists alongside LevelInfo/LevelError. The existing SentrySink keeps working by adapting to the neutral scrubber and mapping LevelWarning to sentry.LevelWarning.

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/sink.go (comments + LevelWarning const), internal/telemetry/scrubber.go + scrubber_test.go (extract/keep neutral helpers), internal/telemetry/sentry.go (adapt BeforeSend to wrap the neutral helpers; keep functional). Do not touch cmd/rally or internal/relay.

Design: Keep the Sink interface signatures unchanged — only wording and the added level value. Factor scrubbing into functions over map[string]string / map[string]map[string]interface{}: collapse home prefixes to ~, drop sensitive keys ENTIRELY (no [scrubbed] placeholder), truncate long strings. sentry.go composes these over sentry.Event so behavior is preserved.

Acceptance: scrubber unit tests cover home-path collapse, dropping sensitive keys with no placeholder, and string truncation on plain maps; go build ./... and go test ./internal/telemetry are green; no "Sentry Issue" wording remains in sink.go. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Add deterministic New Relic attribute builder with budget and priority
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 1.5). The New Relic sink (next lap) needs a deterministic way to turn Rally tags + nested contexts into bounded scalar attributes. Builds on the backend-neutral scrubbing helpers from the previous lap.

Outcome: a new attribute builder (e.g. internal/telemetry/attributes.go) merges FailureEvent/Event Tags and Contexts into a flat map of scalar values (string/number/bool), applies scrubbing, and enforces Rally's local budget: at most 64 attributes, keys < 255 bytes, bounded string values, no nested-map JSON blobs. When over budget it keeps correlation + failure/outcome fields first (relay_id, run_id, try_id, repo, lap_id, runner, role, outcome, failure_category, recovery_classification, agent_state), then deterministic lower-priority context fields, dropping the remainder.

Files & scope: new internal/telemetry/attributes.go + attributes_test.go; may reuse scrubber.go helpers. Do not implement the sink yet.

Design: flatten nested contexts into prefixed keys (e.g. failure_evidence.message, rally.version) but only bounded scalars; never JSON-encode a whole nested map. The priority list must be an explicit stable ordering so dropping is deterministic. Drop (not placeholder) sensitive keys via the neutral scrubber; truncate long strings to the bound.

Acceptance: tests prove scalar-only output; <=64 attributes with keys < 255 bytes; oversized context payloads dropped deterministically rather than JSON-encoded (3.4); correlation/failure fields survive when over budget (3.3); home paths collapsed, and prompt/transcript/log/current_task plus host/user identity keys dropped with NO [scrubbed] placeholder, long strings truncated (1.5). go test ./internal/telemetry green. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Implement NewRelicSink core: transactions, segments, events, errors, flush
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5 sink-side, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 3.5, 3.6). The heart of the migration; uses the attribute builder from the previous lap. Sentry remains the ACTIVE sink until the later cut-over lap — add NewRelicSink alongside, without wiring resolveSink to it. The panic-recovery path is the NEXT lap; a plain deferred Transaction.End is an acceptable placeholder here (note it for lap 4).

Outcome: internal/telemetry/newrelic.go implements NewRelicSink satisfying Sink, backed by github.com/newrelic/go-agent/v3/newrelic. StartSpan creates a New Relic transaction for root relay spans and segments for child run/try spans, preserving Rally span id / parent id as custom attributes (2.3). Span.Finish attaches bounded scrubbed attributes — operation, description, duration, relay/run/try ids, role, runner, outcome, failure & recovery classification — via the attribute builder (2.4). EmitTryLog records exactly one RallyTry custom event (2.5 sink-side). CaptureEvent records RallyDiagnostic with a scalar level (2.6). CaptureFailure calls transaction-scoped Transaction.NoticeError(newrelic.Error{Message,Class,Attributes}) with a stable Class derived from failure categories, and MAY also record a RallyFailure custom event (2.7). Flush uses bounded Application.Shutdown / WaitForConnection so an unreachable endpoint cannot hang exit (2.8). telemetry.Config gains New Relic fields — license key, app name, generic host display name, app-log forwarding/metrics settings, startup-wait and shutdown timeouts — added ADDITIVELY with Sentry fields left intact for now (2.2). Agent config applies ConfigAppLogEnabled/ForwardingEnabled/MetricsEnabled(true) with bounded ConfigAppLogForwardingMaxSamplesStored and decorating off, applied AFTER ConfigFromEnvironment (3.5), plus a generic HostDisplayName with no Rally host/user attributes (3.6).

Files & scope: new internal/telemetry/newrelic.go; internal/telemetry/init.go (add New Relic fields to the Config struct ONLY — do not switch resolveSink yet); go.mod / go.sum (add newrelic v3). Do not touch cmd/rally, internal/relay, or remove Sentry.

Design: keep New Relic types out of the Sink-facing API. Use the attribute builder for every transaction/segment/event/error payload. LevelWarning maps to a New Relic diagnostic attribute, not an error. Apply logging options AFTER ConfigFromEnvironment so env cannot silently change forwarding shape (design Decision 4). Derive a small fixed set of error Class values from stable failure categories.

Acceptance: go build ./... passes with the new dependency; include at least a smoke test that NewRelicSink satisfies Sink and constructs with a disabled/unconfigured config making no network calls. Thorough unit tests are the dedicated sink-tests lap. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Add panic-aware New Relic transaction finish/recovery
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (task 2.9; design Decision 1 panic path and Risks). Because Rally hides transaction ending behind Span.Finish, cfg.ErrorCollector.RecordPanics alone is insufficient. Isolated high-risk control-flow lap.

Outcome: cfg.ErrorCollector.RecordPanics = true is set. A panic-aware finish/recovery path: where a transaction-backed span would defer Transaction.End, instead recover(), notice a newrelic.Error with newrelic.NewStackTrace() and bounded attributes, end the transaction, then re-panic so Rally's existing process/exit semantics are unchanged. Existing string-based panic classification remains only a fallback for agent-output text.

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/newrelic.go (transaction-backed span Finish/recover path). Do not change relay panic handling or process-exit ordering.

Design: the recover+re-panic must preserve the original panic value/stack as seen by upstream recover sites; only New Relic recording is added. Bound attributes via the attribute builder. Ensure there is no double-End on the transaction in the normal (non-panic) path.

Acceptance: a unit test triggers a panic through a transaction-backed span and asserts the transaction is ended, a New Relic error with a stack trace is recorded (fake/isolated app where feasible), and the panic is re-raised. go test ./internal/telemetry green. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: New Relic sink unit tests, app-logging regression tests, volume bounds
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 2.10, 3.7, 3.8). Laps 3-4 added the sink; build the thorough test matrix following existing telemetry test patterns (sink_test.go, scrubber_test.go). Tests must run offline with no real network.

Outcome: unit tests (fake/isolated New Relic app config where feasible) cover transaction/segment creation and Rally span-id attributes; RallyTry/RallyDiagnostic/RallyFailure custom-event payload shape and fixed names; NoticeError attributes and stable error classes; shutdown-timeout behavior on Flush; and no network calls when telemetry is disabled (2.10). Regression tests prove Rally's post-environment config leaves application logging + forwarding + metrics enabled, keeps local decorating off, applies the sample limit, and adds no Application.RecordLog calls or logger integrations in 0.9.1 (3.7). Tests assert event volume stays bounded: one RallyTry per persisted try, bounded spans/segments, bounded app-log samples, and no prompt-line/agent-output custom events (3.8).

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/newrelic_test.go (+ helpers).

Acceptance: go test ./internal/telemetry passes with the new tests; tests run offline. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify New Relic sink phase
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic. Phase verification covering laps 1-5 (tasks 1.2, 1.4, 1.7, 2.1-2.10, 3.1-3.8), before the activation cut-over.

Use the openspec-verify-change skill against migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic, focused on the sink phase. Identify the intended merge target before diffing — ask the user, or read PR metadata / repo docs / branch config / recent history; do NOT assume main — then use git diff <target>...HEAD. Identify the first lap/try of this batch and treat earlier branch work as pre-existing; flag but do not remove it.

Trace the real lines, not just green tests: StartSpan maps to transactions/segments with preserved span ids; EmitTryLog emits exactly one RallyTry; CaptureFailure uses NoticeError with a stable Class; Flush is bounded; the panic path recovers, ends the transaction, then re-panics; the attribute builder enforces the 64-attr / key-length / scalar-value budget and drops sensitive keys with NO [scrubbed] placeholder. Run go test ./internal/telemetry.

Do not rewrite git history. For substantive gaps add focused head laps (laps add head ...) using additive/revert commits; fix only tiny safe one-liners here. Report findings first. Then check off the tasks.md boxes for 1.2, 1.4, 1.7, 2.1-2.10, 3.1-3.8 only for work verified correct and thorough.

tasks.title: Move RallyTry emission after AppendTry
tasks.description: Finish task 2.5 (and the remaining 3.8 gap): move the normal and handoff-only EmitTryLog calls in internal/relay/runner.go so RallyTry is recorded only after store.AppendTry succeeds, and add regression coverage proving failed persistence emits no RallyTry while a persisted try emits exactly one.

tasks.title: Add lap-mismatch diagnostics and real NR regression tripwires
tasks.description: Implement task 1.6 end to end: when a try records wrong_lap_consumed or multi_lap_consumed, emit RallyDiagnostic with level=warning, event_kind=lap_pin_mismatch, and mismatch_reason, and do not report RallyFailure by default. Replace the placeholder-only New Relic regression tests in internal/telemetry/newrelic_coverage_test.go with load-bearing assertions/tripwires for no Application.RecordLog/logger integration and bounded custom-event surfaces.

tasks.title: Cut over activation to New Relic and remove Sentry
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 1.1, 1.3 code parts, 4.1, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 4.6). Keystone lap: the build is green before AND after, but Sentry is removed and New Relic wired here in one coherent change. The New Relic sink already exists from laps 3-4.

Outcome: github.com/getsentry/sentry-go removed from go.mod/go.sum; internal/telemetry/sentry.go and Sentry-specific tests deleted (1.1). DefaultSentryDSN, SENTRY_DSN, the [telemetry] sentry_dsn config field, and Sentry fallback/deprecation branches removed from the code path (1.3 code). internal/telemetry/init.go resolveSink rewritten to the New Relic precedence: RALLY_TELEMETRY=0 -> [telemetry] enabled=false -> NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY -> baked DefaultNewRelicLicenseKey -> no-op; a missing license is a no-op with no network calls and no machine-id file; never falls back to Sentry (4.4, 4.5). telemetry.Config drops Sentry fields, keeps New Relic fields. internal/config.TelemetryConfig (internal/config/config_v2.go) gains enabled *bool, new_relic_app_name, optional new_relic_host_display_name, and removes sentry_dsn (4.1). cmd/rally/main.go: remove the DefaultSentryDSN global; add DefaultNewRelicLicenseKey plus app-name/host-display fields; update telemetryConfigForRelay to populate the New Relic Config (4.3). Mechanical commands (help/version/update) still do not initialize telemetry or create machine-id even with baked credentials (4.6).

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/init.go, delete internal/telemetry/sentry.go + its test, internal/config/config_v2.go, cmd/rally/main.go (globals + telemetryConfigForRelay), go.mod/go.sum. Update/delete cmd/rally/main_test.go references to DefaultSentryDSN just enough to compile — the full activation test matrix is the next lap.

Design: activation precedence is the highest-risk surface — match the spec ordering exactly. Preserve the no-side-effect guarantee: machine-id is created only when a real sink activates (InitWithIdentity). enabled is a *bool so unset differs from false. Removing Sentry must leave no dangling imports. Never read a New Relic license key from tracked config.

Acceptance: go build ./... and go test ./... pass; grep shows no getsentry import, no DefaultSentryDSN, no SENTRY_DSN, no sentry_dsn in the active path; help/version/update create no machine-id file. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Activation test matrix and generated config template
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 4.2, 4.7). After the cut-over, lock the precedence with tests and update the generated config docs.

Outcome: the generated .rally/config.toml [telemetry] section exposes the opt-out discoverably as a commented example (# enabled = false) — never an active enabled = false — and contains no new_relic_license_key field (4.2). Activation tests cover every precedence branch (RALLY_TELEMETRY=0; [telemetry] enabled=false; env NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY overriding baked default; baked default; no key -> no-op), kill-switch behavior, config opt-out, source-build no-op, and that tracked config does not accept New Relic secrets (4.7).

Files & scope: cmd/rally/main.go (generated-config template — the block that currently emits sentry_dsn around line ~514), cmd/rally/main_test.go and/or a new internal/telemetry init test, internal/config tests.

Design: mirror the existing activation test patterns (the old DefaultSentryDSN test in main_test.go). Assert no machine-id file is created on every disabled branch.

Acceptance: go test ./cmd/rally ./internal/telemetry ./internal/config green; generated config contains # enabled = false and no secret field. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Relay: move try-log emission after persistence; lap mismatch becomes warning
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 2.5 relay call sites, 1.6; design Decision 6). Touches the production relay data path in internal/relay/runner.go. The sink already emits RallyTry/RallyDiagnostic.

Outcome: the normal and handoff-only EmitTryLog calls move to AFTER successful store.AppendTry so a failed persistence cannot emit a RallyTry (2.5). Today the normal EmitTryLog at runner.go:~1939 precedes AppendTry at ~1995, and the handoff-only EmitTryLog at ~2408 precedes AppendTry at ~2424 — reorder both (line numbers will drift; locate by call site). Lap-pin mismatches (wrong_lap_consumed / multi_lap_consumed) are reclassified from operator-worthy failures to warning diagnostics: remove lapPinMismatch from the issueWorthy condition (runner.go:~1970) and instead emit a RallyDiagnostic via CaptureEvent with level=warning (telemetry.LevelWarning), event_kind=lap_pin_mismatch, and mismatch_reason=wrong_lap_consumed|multi_lap_consumed (1.6, Decision 6).

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go; internal/relay/runner_failure_telemetry_test.go and/or runner_test.go for the new assertions. Do not change retry/outcome logic — only telemetry emission ordering and the mismatch classification.

Design: moving EmitTryLog must not drop fields gathered before AppendTry — capture tryLogFields, then emit after AppendTry succeeds. Ensure the warning diagnostic still fires on the mismatch path now that it is no longer a CaptureFailure. Keep needs_user and infra-class failures as operator-worthy (unchanged).

Acceptance: new/updated tests prove (a) no RallyTry emission when AppendTry fails, and (b) a lap mismatch produces a RallyDiagnostic with level=warning, event_kind=lap_pin_mismatch, mismatch_reason, and NOT a CaptureFailure/RallyFailure. go test ./internal/relay green. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify activation cut-over and relay telemetry changes
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic. Slice-boundary verification covering laps 6-8 (tasks 1.1, 1.3 code, 4.1-4.7, 2.5 relay, 1.6) — all runtime/behavior changes before release wiring and docs. High-risk activation path and production relay data path.

Use the openspec-verify-change skill against the change, focused on activation + cut-over + relay. Identify the intended merge target before diffing (ask the user, or read PR metadata / repo docs / branch config / recent history; do NOT assume main) and use git diff <target>...HEAD. Identify the batch's first lap/try and treat earlier branch work as pre-existing.

Trace the real lines: resolveSink precedence ordering matches the spec exactly; no Sentry import/DSN/ldflag remains in the active path; machine-id is not created for help/version/update or any disabled branch; EmitTryLog now follows AppendTry at BOTH call sites; lap mismatch emits a warning RallyDiagnostic, not a failure. Run go test ./cmd/rally ./internal/config ./internal/telemetry ./internal/relay.

Do not rewrite git history; add focused head laps for substantive gaps using additive/revert commits, fixing only tiny safe one-liners here. Report findings first. Then check off the tasks.md boxes for 1.1, 1.3 (code parts), 4.1-4.7, 2.5, and 1.6 verified correct and thorough.

tasks.title: Release wiring: GoReleaser ldflags and workflow secret gate
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 5.1, 5.2). The operational secret RALLY_NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY and the variable RALLY_NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME are already configured in GitHub.

Outcome: .goreleaser.yaml ldflags change from -X main.DefaultSentryDSN={{ .Env.RALLY_SENTRY_DSN }} to -X main.DefaultNewRelicLicenseKey={{ .Env.RALLY_NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY }} (5.1, around line ~18). .github/workflows/release.yml passes RALLY_NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY from secrets (replacing RALLY_SENTRY_DSN at line ~46) and fails BEFORE GoReleaser when building a not-yet-existing release with an empty New Relic secret (5.2).

Files & scope: .goreleaser.yaml, .github/workflows/release.yml. Do NOT bump VERSION here — that is gated to a later lap.

Design: the empty-secret gate must run only on the not-yet-existing-release path (mirror the existing release-exists no-op check) so re-runs for an existing release still no-op. Keep the secret out of logs.

Acceptance: goreleaser config references only New Relic; the workflow fails fast on an empty secret for a new release; no RALLY_SENTRY_DSN remains. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Docs: README telemetry section and AGENTS.md observability
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 5.3, 5.4, and the docs portion of 1.3).

Outcome: README telemetry docs describe the New Relic Go APM behavior, env/baked credential precedence, [telemetry] enabled = false, RALLY_TELEMETRY=0, data sent, data intentionally NOT sent, and the hard Sentry cutover (5.3); no normative current-Sentry telemetry docs remain (1.3 docs). AGENTS.md observability section switches from the Sentry CLI investigation guidance to New Relic guidance, noting Sentry issue IDs only as historical pre-0.9.1 references (5.4).

Files & scope: README.md (telemetry section), AGENTS.md (the section currently headed "Observability (Sentry)").

Acceptance: docs match the implemented activation precedence and privacy posture; no live Sentry instructions remain. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Retire obsolete Sentry planning references and align 0.10.0 plans
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 5.7, 5.8). Keep Rally's OpenSpec planning coherent after the migration.

Outcome: the active release-0-10-0-reliability-and-model-routing artifacts are updated so telemetry language builds on New Relic Go APM / backend-neutral terminology instead of reintroducing Sentry Issues or fallback behavior (5.7). Obsolete Sentry planning references are retired or rewritten — openspec/changes/enrich-sentry-coverage/draft.md and openspec/next-up.md — preserving only backend-neutral / New Relic concepts such as native panic/error capture (5.8).

Files & scope: openspec/changes/release-0-10-0-reliability-and-model-routing/**, openspec/changes/enrich-sentry-coverage/draft.md, openspec/next-up.md. Do NOT modify the migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic specs/tasks themselves.

Design: where a Sentry concept maps cleanly to New Relic (Issue -> operator-worthy failure / NoticeError; breadcrumb -> custom event), rewrite rather than delete. Drop guidance that would steer future work back to Sentry.

Acceptance: no active 0.10.0 plan reintroduces Sentry semantics; the panic/error-capture intent survives in New Relic terms. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Bump VERSION to 0.9.1
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic (tasks 5.5, 5.6). Must come only AFTER the release secret gate (the release-wiring lap) is in place.

Outcome: internal/buildinfo/VERSION set to 0.9.1; main.Version remains "dev" in source (5.5). Add the release-checklist note that RALLY_NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY (secret) and RALLY_NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME (variable) are already configured in GitHub — verify they are still present before cutting 0.9.1, keep the license key secret, and do not push tags manually (5.6).

Files & scope: internal/buildinfo/VERSION; the release checklist (README releasing section or AGENTS.md releasing section).

Design: do NOT create or push a vX.Y.Z tag — the auto-tag workflow handles tagging from the VERSION file. Confirm the main.Version source value is unchanged.

Acceptance: VERSION reads 0.9.1; main.Version still "dev"; the checklist note is present. Do not check tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Final full-change verification for the New Relic migration
tasks.description:
OpenSpec change: migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic. Final verification across all phases (tasks 6.1-6.5 and the whole change).

Use the openspec-verify-change skill against migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic and inspect the whole diff. Identify the intended merge target before diffing (ask the user, or read PR metadata / repo docs / branch config / recent history; do NOT assume main) and use git diff <target>...HEAD. Identify the batch's first lap/try and treat earlier branch work as pre-existing unless the user asks to include it.

Run the change's verification commands: go test ./internal/telemetry ./internal/config ./cmd/rally (6.1); the broader relay telemetry tests exercising SetTelemetry, failure capture, limit diagnostics, recovery needs_user, lap-mismatch diagnostics, and prompt-size fields (6.2); go test ./... (6.3); openspec validate migrate-telemetry-to-new-relic --strict (6.4). Manually inspect go.mod, go.sum, .goreleaser.yaml, .github/workflows/release.yml, README, and AGENTS.md diffs for leaked credentials, accidental Sentry release wiring, or log-forwarding exposure (6.5). Confirm internal/buildinfo/VERSION is 0.9.1 and main.Version is "dev".

Do not rewrite git history; add focused head laps for any substantive gap (fix only tiny safe one-liners here). Report findings first. Then check off the remaining tasks.md boxes (5.x, 6.x, and any earlier box not yet ticked) only for work verified correct and thorough.
